No Poverty.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to health, well-being, or related medical applications.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides advanced technical and problem-solving skills essential for software engineering, directly supporting quality tertiary education (4.3) and relevant skills for employment (4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course emphasizes high-efficiency algorithms, which can indirectly contribute to energy efficiency (Target 7.3) by reducing computational resource and power consumption.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly prepares students for high-value software engineering roles, fostering technological upgrading, innovation, and increased economic productivity (Target 8.2) through efficient problem-solving.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Algorithms are fundamental to technological innovation, scientific research, and upgrading industrial capabilities (Target 9.5), forming the backbone of modern digital infrastructure and problem-solving.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to reducing inequalities based on income, age, sex, disability, race, or other status.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to sustainable cities, housing, transport, or urban planning.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's emphasis on high-efficiency algorithms promotes the efficient use of computational resources, indirectly aligning with sustainable resource management and efficiency (Target 12.2).

Climate Action.reason: High-efficiency algorithms can reduce energy consumption in computing, offering a very indirect and minor contribution to climate change mitigation efforts (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to marine life or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course content on algorithms and programming has no direct or indirect connection to peace, justice, or institutional strength.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course explicitly aims to cultivate teamwork and collaboration through group projects, which are foundational skills for effective partnerships (Target 17.17), even if limited to a classroom setting.
